title: Add friction resolve workflow
goal: Turn the Grabowski friction ledger from an accumulating observation log into a resolvable decision ledger with explicit closeout evidence for recurring failure classes.

acceptance.assertion: A friction resolve or triage path records event_id, decision, status, evidence_ref, resolved_by, closed_at and non-claims without rewriting raw historical events unsafely.
Recurring Platform-Filter, policy-gate, environment-tooling and actionable-failure classes can be closed or deliberately deferred with a canonical decision and evidence.
friction_summary separates unresolved, resolved, superseded and deferred events so stale already-fixed findings do not dominate next-decision output.
Resolvable friction can create or link Bureau tasks without implying task readiness, merge readiness or owner approval.
Focused tests cover resolving one event, resolving a class, duplicate closeout, invalid event id, deferred-with-reason and summary counts.

metadata.source: User requested phases 1-4 from the 2026-07-08 Grabowski optimization plan be registered as Bureau tasks.
metadata.live_basis: Recent friction summary had 39 events with 34 unresolved decision-required events; some findings are already technically obsolete after recovery/tool-surface changes.
metadata.verification_note: Grabowski PR #152 introduced the append-only, event- and failure-class-bound friction closeout ledger. The live runtime at fbe9a11b9cf457f25756b0a4c8559b2cfbd6b46e reports two valid decisions, no duplicate or conflicting closeouts, intact event bindings and separate unresolved/resolved counts.

validated_acceptance.resolve-record: The closeout schema records event_id, classified failure_class, decision, status, evidence_ref, resolved_by, closed_at, optional bureau_task_id and explicit non-claims in an append-only decisions ledger; raw events remain unchanged.
validated_acceptance.class-closeout: The resolve API requires exactly one event_id or failure_class selector, supports bounded recurring-class closeout, reports remaining targets, and accepts resolved, superseded, deferred, accepted_risk, wont_fix and linked_to_task decisions with evidence and reasons.
validated_acceptance.summary-noise-reduced: The live summary separates 39 unresolved, 2 resolved and 9 legacy-resolved recent events. The two evidence-bound closeouts no longer count as decision-required noise.
validated_acceptance.bureau-bridge: Closeouts accept a validated optional bureau_task_id for linked_to_task decisions while retaining non-claims for task readiness, merge readiness and policy exception.
validated_acceptance.tests: PR #152 passed the full local suite with 1002 tests and GitHub validate (3.10)/(3.12). Focused friction tests cover single-event closeout, failure-class deferral, bounded class closeout, duplicate and conflicting decisions, invalid identifiers, reason requirements, task linking and summary counts.

resolved_events.decision: Typed PR create path was fixed and proved by creating PR #153 on the exact published head.
Captain now reads repository merge policy, selected squash, merged PR #153, and verified the remote target state.

validation.github_ci: validate (3.10) and validate (3.12) succeeded for Grabowski PR #152
validation.local_suite: 1002 tests passed for the implementation PR
